thesis: Wolfspeed is the leading pure-play silicon carbide (SiC) semiconductor company, vertically integrated from crystal growth to power devices. A recent $476M capital raise and 25% debt reduction have materially improved the balance sheet and extended runway. However, revenue remains in decline (-18.9% YoY), gross margins are deeply negative, and the stock has risen 560%+ from lows in what appears to be a short-squeeze dynamic (RSI 83+). The AI/data center power opportunity is real, but the current price embeds a flawless turnaround execution that is unlikely to materialize smoothly. Hold existing positions; do not chase.

drivers: AI/data center power revenue grows to 30%+ of revenue mix by FY28
Mohawk Valley 200mm fab reaches 50%+ utilization, driving positive gross margins
Automotive SiC adoption accelerates with Chinese EV and Tier-1 partnerships
Continued debt paydown creates path to profitability by FY28

risks: SiC commoditization accelerates; pricing pressure crushes margins
EV demand weakness persists; revenue continues declining
Additional dilutive capital raises required within 12–18 months
Short-squeeze reversal drives violent technical selloff

base_case.assumptions: Revenue stabilizes at $600–700M annually with modest growth. Gross margins turn slightly positive by late FY27. EBITDA breakeven by FY29. Valuation converges toward analyst consensus (~$40 target). Additional capital raises cause moderate dilution.

catalysts.event: Q4 FY26 Earnings (expected) — revenue guidance for FY27
Potential analyst/investor day with Mohawk Valley update
Q1 FY27 Earnings — first post-restructuring clean quarter

key_risks: Continued cash burn and potential dilutive capital raises
SiC commoditization and intensifying competition from Onsemi, Infineon, STMicro, Chinese vendors
Automotive EV demand weakness in key markets
Mohawk Valley 200mm ramp delays or yield challenges
Short-squeeze reversal creating violent technical downside
